definitions.text: Arriving upon or on top of (speaking of a physical or metaphorical movement).
examples: My cat just jumped onto the keyboard.
A new drug has just come onto the market.
The thought-police were onto my plans of world domination.

definitions.text: Being an onto function with a codomain of (see below).
examples: The exponential function maps the set of real numbers onto the set of positive real numbers.

definitions.text: Attaining each of the values in its codomain; having its image equal to its codomain.
examples: Considered as a function on the real numbers, the exponential function is not onto, as it never takes on values less than or equal to zero.
